15 defendant Terri Shultis sent a letter to plaintiff, on behalf of defendant Golden Gate
, 16 University, dated November 28, 2007, informing him of the outcome of defendant
17 Golden Gate University’s investigation of complaints against him by students. The
18 complaints consisted of two allegations, that (1) plaintiff made derogatory remarks about
19 one or more of the students’ ethnic backgrounds, thereby creating a hostile environment,
20 and (2) that plaintiff made specific remarks of a sexual nature regarding masturbation,
21 and that he invited students to his home in such a way as to give the appearance that his
22 motives included establishing a romantic relationship with them. Defendants deny all
23 other allegations stated in paragraph 19 of the SAC. ,
24 20. Defendants admit the allegations stated in paragraph 20 of the SAC only to the extent
25 that the aforementioned letter to plaintiff, dated November 28, 2007, stated that there was
26 “sufficient evidence” to support the conclusion that plaintiff had made derogatory and
27 , unacceptable comments to individual students. Defendants deny all other allegations
28 Stated in paragraph 20 of the SAC.

3 FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
4 AS AND FOR A FIRST, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS ALLEGE:
5 That the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against these
6 || answering defendants,
7 SECOND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
8 AS AND FOR A SECOND, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS
9 || ALLEGE: .
10 That the sole cause of action for Slander does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of
11 | action against these answering defendants.
12 THIRD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
13 AS AND FOR A THIRD, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS ALLEGE:
14 Plaintiff's cause of action is barred by reason of the provisions of California Code of Civil .
15 | Procedure sections 335.1, 337, 337.1 » 338, 339, 340, and 343.
16 FOURTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
17 AS AND FOR A FOURTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS
18 | ALLEGE:
19 Plaintiff failed to mitigate his damages,
20 |” FIFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
21 AS AND FOR A FIFTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS
22 || ALLEGE:
23 Should plaintiff recover non-economic damages against any defendant, the liability for non-
24 || economic damages is limited to the degree of fault and several liability of said defendant pursuant to
25 || Civil Code section 1431.2 and a separate, several judgment shall be rendered against said defendant
26 || based upon said defendant's degree of fault and several liability.
27h
28 | ///

 
! . SIXTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
2 AS AND FOR A SIXTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS ALLEGE:
3 As a further, Separate, affirmative defense, defendants allege that it is entitled to a set-off of any
4 || damages recovered by plaintiff.
5 . SEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
6 AS AND FOR A SEVENTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS
7 | ALLEGE:
8 That their conduct, of which plaintiff complains, was privileged and that each cause of action
9 |] and/or factual allegations alleged in the SAC is barred by the doctrine of privilege. )
10 EIGHTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
I] AS AND FORA EIGHTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS
12 | ALLEGE:
13 | That any conduct or actions undertaken by or on behalf of the defendants regarding plaintiff
14 || were done in good faith, without mal ice, and pursuant to the reasonable conduct of the defendants’
15 || business, and were thus privileged.
16 NINTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
17 ' ASAND FORA NINTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS ALLEGE:
18 That plaintifP’s claims are barred pursuant to equitable doctrines, including but not limited to
19 | estoppel, waiver, and unclean hands.
20 TENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
21 AS AND FOR A TENTH, SEPARATE AND DISTINCT DEFENSE, DEFENDANTS
22 || ALLEGE:
23 That any awarded punitive damages if sought by plaintiff would violate the due process and
24 jl exclusive fine causes of the Fifth, Eighth, and Fourteenth Amendments of the United States
25 || Constitution, as well as the Constitution of the State of California.
